sourceMovie.title: The Bourne Legacy
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Bourne Legacy for the same reason it broke through in 2012: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Tony Gilroy keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Skyfall
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Skyfall (2012) belongs to the same conversation as The Bourne Legacy — both anchor their action in thriller emotion that doesn't let go. The tense undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Tenet
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Bourne Legacy's action stayed with you, Tenet (2020) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The thriller through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Mission: Impossible - Ghost Protocol
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where The Bourne Legacy excels in action, Mission: Impossible - Ghost Protocol (2011) doubles down on thriller to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Nobody
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Nobody (2021) is the natural next step after The Bourne Legacy: same action DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and thriller elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Mission: Impossible III
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of The Bourne Legacy who respond to its action will find Mission: Impossible III (2006) immediately familiar. Both films share a thriller sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Salt
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason The Bourne Legacy fans keep landing on Salt (2010) — the action is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: The Transporter
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Transporter draws from the same action well as The Bourne Legacy, channeling thriller in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2002 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Angel Has Fallen
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Angel Has Fallen (2019) captures the action appeal of The Bourne Legacy without simply imitating it. The thriller streak running through both films is what puts Angel Has Fallen at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Anna
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action appeal of The Bourne Legacy — that tense edge, the thriller — translates cleanly into Anna (2019). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: The Kingdom
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind The Kingdom (2007) clearly understood what makes action work — the same quality that keeps The Bourne Legacy rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Bourne Legacy?
faq.answer: Try Skyfall, Tenet, Mission: Impossible - Ghost Protocol—each shares DNA with The Bourne Legacy while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Bourne Legacy?
faq.answer: The Bourne Legacy (2012) is best described as Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Bourne Legacy part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Bourne Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
